list.name: patient
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT patient_id FROM cgm_readings ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Ingestion Rate (rows/min, wall-clock)
panels.description: Rows actually written to cgm_readings per minute, bucketed by ingested_at (when the consumer wrote them) -- not by the sensor's own event time, which is historical AZT1D replay data from Dec 2023-Apr 2024.

targets.rawSql: SELECT time_bucket('1 minute', ingested_at) AS "time", count(*) AS rows_ingested FROM cgm_readings WHERE $__timeFilter(ingested_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Glucose Trace -- Patient $patient
panels.description: Most recent 500 readings for the selected patient, by sensor event time. Ignores the dashboard time-range picker deliberately -- AZT1D's own timestamps are historical, so this always shows the latest available trace regardless of picker.

targets.rawSql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT time AS "time", glucose_value AS glucose FROM cgm_readings WHERE patient_id = '$patient' ORDER BY time DESC LIMIT 500) sub ORDER BY "time" ASC;

panels.title: DLQ Health (events/min by failure class)
panels.description: cgm-parse-errors (structural failures), cgm-dlq (conflicting same-timestamp groups, human review), cgm-implausible (CGM <40 or >400 mg/dL). See docs/QUESTIONS.md 2026-07-13 entry.

targets.rawSql: SELECT time_bucket('1 minute', received_at) AS "time", topic AS metric, count(*) AS events FROM dlq_events WHERE $__timeFilter(received_at) GROUP BY 1, topic ORDER BY 1;
